hyperedges.label: Morsel Infrastructure Stack
hyperedges.rationale: PostgreSQL container, Spring Boot datasource config, and production overrides together form the data infrastructure layer of the Morsel application

hyperedges.label: Morsel Security Configuration
hyperedges.rationale: JWT authentication, account lockout policy, and CORS configuration collectively define the application's security boundary across both dev and prod environments

nodes.label: JPA Schema Validation
nodes.rationale: ddl-auto: validate ensures database schema matches JPA entities without auto-DDL, preventing accidental schema changes in production-like environments

nodes.label: RFC 7807 Problem Details
nodes.rationale: RFC 7807 Problem Details for HTTP APIs standardizes error responses with machine-readable error codes and typed details, replacing ad-hoc error bodies

nodes.label: Java Virtual Threads
nodes.rationale: Virtual threads (Project Loom) enable lightweight concurrency, allowing each request to run on its own virtual thread without the overhead of OS thread pooling

nodes.label: Mailpit Development Mail
nodes.rationale: Mailpit on localhost:1025 provides a disposable SMTP server for development email testing without sending real emails

links.rationale: The PostgreSQL container (postgres:18-alpine) with database 'morsel' on port 5432 is the database instance that the Spring Boot datasource URL (jdbc:postgresql://localhost:5432/morsel) connects to
